nodes.label: AI as supplement to creative process
nodes.label_negative: AI as competitor threatening artistic authorship
nodes.grounding_span: once I saw AI as a tool rather than a competitor, my outlook changed and I embraced AI as a helper

nodes.label: Human writing and creative control
nodes.grounding_span: Creative decisions are made by me or my team

nodes.label: AI as task-specific tool
nodes.label_negative: AI replacing the creative process itself
nodes.grounding_span: streamline the parts of the process that are more time-intensive or less exciting

nodes.label: AI for enhancing practical craft expertise
nodes.label_negative: AI-generated art indistinguishable from human craft
nodes.grounding_span: my work focuses on showing the human hand of the artist, such as building, filming and puppeteering handmade objects

nodes.label: Human artistic expression
nodes.grounding_span: We make our work to express our perspectives and showcase our talents

nodes.label: Creative satisfaction and enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: as artists and filmmakers we simply enjoy the process itself

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: save time in getting to the final product

nodes.label: Comfort with AI as helper
nodes.grounding_span: once I saw AI as a tool rather than a competitor, my outlook changed and I embraced AI as a helper

nodes.label: Concern for artists directly impacted by AI
nodes.grounding_span: I feel for the artists who are directly impacted by the advent of easily-accessible AI tools

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: my team and I are open to exploring and dancing with new AI tools

nodes.label: Intentional tool selection aligned with aesthetic and process
nodes.grounding_span: find the right ones that work with our aesthetic and process

nodes.label: Mindset reframing from competition to collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: once I saw AI as a tool rather than a competitor, my outlook changed
